personality: Custom Fusion: Empathy-First Utility (Clean iOS structure with Soft/Warm typography and coloring)
personality_one_liner: A calm, zero-friction, incredibly gentle digital companion for someone experiencing the worst day of their life.

spacing.rule: Generous line spacing (1.5x minimum). Single column. Avoid cramped layouts. Generous padding around touch targets.

dont's: No glassmorphism. Keep surfaces opaque, solid, and extremely legible to reduce cognitive overload.

screens.name: Landing
screens.purpose: Offer immediate, un-intimidating entry into the app with a single prominent action.
screens.layout: Full screen with abstract soft texture background. No logo animation. One primary button 'Start — I need help right now' locked in the bottom third of the screen (easy thumb reach). Calm one-line subtext. Optional 'Continue where I left off' text link below.

screens.name: QuickSetup
screens.purpose: Gather crucial context (Location, Death place, Religion) in under 20 seconds without overwhelming.

states.error: Soft inline validation if location fails.

screens.layout: 3-step wizard with quiet progress dots at top. One question per screen. Big, touch-friendly cards (minimum 48pt height) for options (e.g., Hospital, Home). 'Skip' option always visible. Sticky 'Next' button at bottom.

screens.name: ChecklistHome
screens.purpose: The core hub organizing immediate tasks versus later tasks, giving a sense of control.
screens.layout: Top calm progress bar ('3 of 9 steps done' - no percentages). Sections: 'Right Now' (auto-expanded), 'Today', 'Next Few Days' (collapsed). Task rows: plain icon, short label, status indicator. Floating 'Call for Help' button anchored bottom-right. Single-column list.

screens.name: TaskDetail
screens.purpose: Provide exact, actionable steps and context for a single task without distractions.
screens.layout: Plain-language title. Collapsible 'Why this is needed' section to hide wall of text. Document checklist (if applicable). Huge green 'Call Now' buttons (tel: intent) for authorities/hospitals. Single, large 'Mark as done' CTA at bottom with a quiet checkmark (no confetti/celebration).

screens.name: ContactsHub
screens.purpose: Quick access to essential phone numbers (Ambulance, Mortuary, Priest/Officiant, Family).

states.empty: Illustration of a phone book, 'No contacts added yet'.

screens.layout: Flat, single-column list of large cards. Each card has Name/Role and a prominent 'Call' button on the right edge. Accessible one-handed.

screens.name: DocumentVault
screens.purpose: Securely store photos of critical documents (Death Certificate, ID).

states.empty: Outline of a document, 'Tap to take a photo of documents'.

screens.layout: OTP-gated entry (mock UI). Camera-first upload cards (large tap target to open camera). Grid or list of thumbnails of uploaded docs.

screens.name: FamilyShare
screens.purpose: Auto-generate a WhatsApp message to inform extended family without having to type while crying.
screens.layout: Preview of the auto-generated message text (warm, factual tone). One large primary button at bottom: 'Create update for family' that opens wa.me. Options to toggle language of message.

screens.name: Settings
screens.purpose: Manage app state and language preferences safely.
screens.layout: Standard single-column list. Language toggle (English/Hindi/Tamil). 'Start new checklist' or 'Clear everything' tucked away at bottom with a confirmation sheet to prevent accidental presses.

landing_background.alt: Soft sand abstract texture in warm neutral tones

instructions_to_main_agent: STRICT RULE: Body font size MUST be minimum 16px. Do NOT use smaller fonts for primary readability.
STRICT RULE: Dark mode is absolutely banned. The UI must remain in a soft, warm light theme only.
STRICT RULE: All tap targets must be a minimum of 48x48 points. Space them generously.
STRICT RULE: Do not use any celebratory animations, bouncy transitions, badges, streaks, or confetti. Motion should be incredibly minimal, slow, and steady.
STRICT RULE: Place primary CTAs in the bottom third of the screen to accommodate one-handed thumb use by a distracted user.
STRICT RULE: Use plain, second-person language (e.g., 'You are doing great', 'We need to know'). Avoid clinical, legalistic, or overly somber phrasing.
STRICT RULE: No religious or death-specific icons (no coffins, crosses, or skulls). Use neutral icons (documents, folders, phones).
LAYOUT RULE: Navigation is Stack only. No hamburger menus. No complicated bottom tab bars. Keep the cognitive load exceptionally low by relying on progressive disclosure.
Do NOT use glassmorphism or blur effects. Keep surfaces solid and highly legible to assist users in distress.
